title: Release Assets Manifest
description: Embedded SRI-integrity manifest for release artifacts. One block per tool: identifies the upstream release tag and the SRI integrity string (sha256/384/512) of every asset that release published. Consumers verify downloads against these pins before installing or republishing. The release's checksums.txt asset stays sha256-hex (the ecosystem `shasum -c` convention) — this manifest is the internal pin, not the transport format. Producers (e.g. socket-btm) update this file when cutting a new release.

$schema.description: Pointer back to this schema for editor hints.

$comment.description: Free-form note. Conventionally explains how to bump entries.

tag.description: Full release tag, including the tool prefix (e.g. `lief-20260507-76c1796`).

checksums.description: Asset filename → SRI integrity string (`sha256-<base64>`; sha384/sha512 accepted). Keys are typically flat filenames; some manifests use relative paths (e.g. `./model/model.onnx`) when the producer ships nested artifacts. The release's checksums.txt asset carries the sha256-hex form of the same digest — the two formats are re-derivable from each other, never hand-maintained separately.
